item.title: Intesa’s bid for MPS restores some sanity to Italy’s M&A scene
item.description: An Intesa victory might be appealing to Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni

item.title: Wall Street stocks rebound after AI-led rout
item.description: Buyers step in after Friday fall that rattled global markets

item.title: London mansion with mystery owner close to £190mn sale
item.description: The Holme, set in four acres in Regent’s Park, previously sold for £139mn in 2024

item.title: We need to learn how to argue with AI
item.description: Putting humans in the loop is pointless if they simply rubber-stamp authoritative-sounding information

item.title: WhatsApp accuses NSO of fresh Pegasus targeting
item.description: Court filing argues that the Israeli spyware group breached a permanent US court ban

item.title: Berlin pulls plug on Franco-German fighter jet
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz proposes scaling back €100bn defence project to co-operation on ‘combat cloud’

item.title: FCA seeks High Court injunction against Neil Woodford over new online service
item.description: UK financial regulator alleges fund manager is providing unauthorised investment advice

item.title: Uber’s pursuit of Delivery Hero faces fresh challenge from Saudi start-up
item.description: Riyadh-based Ninja is considering making bid for parts of German group’s Middle East business

item.title: Incyte strikes up to $2bn deal for blood disorder biotech
item.description: Midsized drug manufacturers are supplementing pipelines and scaling up in competition with Big Pharma

item.title: Switzerland weighs Franco-Italian alternative to US air defences
item.description: SAMP-T emerges as strong option for Bern as Raytheon’s Patriot system deliveries delayed for at least 5 years

item.title: Canada tries to edge back into US trade talks with Trumpian sales pitch
item.description: ‘Fortress North America’ is Ottawa’s response to being cut out of negotiations on new pact with US and Mexico

item.title: Five ways to boost your motoring experience
item.description: A life-saving jump-starter – plus other kit for the road

item.title: Blackstone looks to sell $2bn of stakes in private investment funds
item.description: One of the biggest deals of its kind will test investor appetite for ageing private equity vehicles

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX courts retail investors
item.description: Plus, redemption woes at Partners Group, shorting call centre stocks and Le Parc at Tate Modern

item.title: Tate & Lyle agrees to £2.7bn takeover from US rival Ingredion
item.description: Take private deal comes as UK ingredients group struggles with subdued consumer spending

item.title: Intesa gatecrashes rival’s bid for Monte dei Paschi with €30.6bn offer
item.description: Italy’s largest bank says takeover would result in significant cost savings

item.title: UK AI start-up PhysicsX hits $2.4bn valuation following Temasek-led deal
item.description: London-based group raises $300mn to become one of the UK’s most valuable AI groups

item.title: FirstFT: AI-powered espionage triggers Putin camera scare
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Iran deal and UK bank taxes

item.title: United Airlines boss puts Rolls-Royce “in the doghouse” over contract spat
item.description: Clash comes amid industry frustrations over engine delivery delays

item.title: The World Cup offers pubs a chance to change the story
item.description: Tournament gives the industry a chance to pull in more punters and pull more pints

item.title: We must not grant AI agents legal personhood
item.description: What kind of sanctions could keep a non-human corporation in check?

item.title: European drivers cut back on fuel as energy price shock bites
item.description: Eurozone records largest year-on-year decline in fuel sales since October 2023

item.title: New AI espionage powers trigger Putin camera scare
item.description: Russia paused surveillance system after killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader exposed how AI can be used on CCTV data to target enemies

item.title: OECD chief urges governments not to go it alone on digital taxation
item.description: Mathias Cormann warns against fragmented approach as more countries consider duties on tech giants

item.title: Hg-backed Gen II targets $6bn sale
item.description: New York-based group in preliminary talks to target sales process in second half of this year

item.title: Software buyout deals collapse to lowest level since pandemic after AI rout
item.description: Value of private equity software acquisitions struck in the first five months of the year tumbles to $50bn

item.title: SpaceX IPO will not break capital markets but add to strains
item.description: Offering will widen the valuation gap between the constituents of indices and the companies outside them

item.title: Are people really making $500k a month on ShopMy?
item.description: An American online fashion marketplace has transformed the influencer economy. Do the numbers check out?

item.title: America is remaking football in its image
item.description: A flood of dollars has made the US one of the biggest forces in the sport

item.title: Santander chief Ana Botín: UK bank taxes make ‘no economic sense’
item.description: Boss of EU’s biggest bank criticises policy of singling out sector for extra levies
